items.text:
1. `objective.json` schema (from P8) in use: `create_workstream` writes a
validated `objective.json` (`{slug, title, status, text}`) alongside
state.json. `objective.md` is immediately regenerated as a view.
`state.json`'s `workstream.objective_artifact` is set to `"objective.json"`.
(Evidence: `lume new` creates objective.json; `lume schema objective` matches.)

2. `_render_objective(doc)` helper in `workstream.py` writes `objective.md`
from an objective doc. Rendered format:
```
---
slug: <slug>
status: <status>
---
# <title>
<text>
```
(Evidence: test asserts regenerated file contains title heading and text.)

3. `Workstream.set_status()` loads `objective.json`, updates `status`,
saves validated `objective.json`, then regenerates `objective.md`.
No longer reads or writes raw frontmatter from objective.md.
(Evidence: grep shows no `frontmatter.parse` / `frontmatter.render` call in
set_status; test asserts objective.json status field updated.)

4. `Workstream.objective_line()` returns the title from
`self._state["workstream"]["title"]` — no disk read required.
(Evidence: grep shows no file read in objective_line.)

5. `Repository._workstream_dirs()` discovers workstream directories by the
presence of `state.json` (not `objective.md` or `objective.json`).
(Evidence: grep; test with a ws_dir containing state.json but no objective.md
is still enumerated.)

6. `Repository.workstream(slug)` validates the slug by checking
`state.json` exists in the candidate directory, not `objective.md`.
(Evidence: test.)

7. `migrate.py` extended: for each workstream, if `objective.json` is
absent, read the prose body from `objective.md`, construct and save
`objective.json`, regenerate `objective.md` as a view, and update
`state.json`'s `objective_artifact` field. Idempotent.
(Evidence: running `lume migrate` twice produces identical objective.json.)

8. All three existing workstreams migrated: `objective.json` present,
`objective.md` regenerated as a view, `state.json` `objective_artifact`
updated. No machine field read from objective.md by the engine after this.
(Evidence: `lume migrate` run; grep lume/ shows no objective.md machine reads
outside migrate.py.)

9. Full suite green; ≥ 6 new tests covering: objective.json created on `new`,
objective.md regenerated as view, set_status updates objective.json,
objective_line from state, workstream discovery via state.json,
migrate produces valid objective.json. No tests removed.
(Evidence: test run 0 failures.)

dod.preamble:
Replace objective.md as the authored source for workstream identity and objective
prose with a schema-validated `objective.json`. Objective.md becomes a derived
view, regenerated by the engine. No machine field is read from objective.md after
this change.

Changes confined to `workstream.py`, `repository.py`, and `migrate.py`.

handback:
Ready for review. Key changes:
- `workstream.py`: `_load_objective`, `_save_objective`, `_render_objective`,
  updated `set_status`, updated `objective_line`, updated `objective_path` property.
- `repository.py`: discovery and slug check use `state.json`; `create_workstream`
  writes objective.json + renders objective.md.
- `migrate.py`: `_objective_prose`, `migrate_objective` added; `migrate_all`
  now calls `migrate_objective`; `objective_artifact` set to `"objective.json"`.
- All three workstreams now have `objective.json` on disk.

Verify: `grep -rn "objective.md" .lume/engine/lume/` confirms the only reads
of objective.md are in `migrate.py` (explicit legacy path) and one CLI hint
message; `workstream.py` only writes it.
self_review:
All 9 DoD items complete. Implementation is clean: `workstream.py` adds
`_load_objective`, `_save_objective`, `_render_objective` as a coherent triple
(load → validate → save → render), `set_status` uses them, `objective_line`
reads from state_doc with no disk I/O. `repository.py` discovery and slug
validation both pivot to `state.json`. `migrate.py` adds `migrate_objective`
(idempotent, prose-preserving) and calls it from `migrate_all`.

One test premise needed a fix mid-iteration: `test_migrate_regenerates_objective_md_view`
was initially written assuming migrate would overwrite a pre-corrupted
objective.md, but migrate reads FROM objective.md as its source on first run,
so corrupting before migration just corrupts the migration. Rewrote the test
to assert the correct thing: after migrate, the rendered view contains the
expected slug/status/title fields. This was a test design error, not an
implementation error.

194 tests passing. 13 new tests in `test_objective_json.py`.
